snapshot: Reuters' Asia-markets piece had Nikkei futures at 50,870, implying a drop of more than 2,500 points from Friday's 53,373 cash close.
Reuters' Tokyo outlook calls for another sharp decline, with a 50,000-52,000 range and even a test below 51,000 on the table.
USD/JPY touched 160.47, while Reuters' weekly FX range is 158-162. Intervention risk is now part of the opening setup.
Brent reached $115.73 and WTI $102.77. High oil keeps resources and tankers supported, but weighs on almost everything else.
S&P 500 futures were down 0.6% and Nasdaq futures down 0.7%, so the pre-open backdrop for Japan remains heavily risk-off.

drivers.driver: Escalating Gulf conflict and another leg higher in oil
drivers.why_today: Houthi attacks widened the conflict, sending Brent into the $115 area and WTI above $102. In Japan, resources, fertilizers and tankers remain the clearest relative winners.

drivers.driver: USD/JPY back above 160 with intervention risk rising
drivers.why_today: Dollar/yen has pushed back through 160. For Japanese equities that means import-cost pressure matters more than the usual exporter support.

drivers.driver: Friday after-close capital actions and earnings revisions
drivers.why_today: AMI, Japan Post, TPR and Shikoku Bank all released clean buyback, cancellation, dividend or guidance catalysts that can still matter at Monday's open.

headline_inventory.headline: Reuters' Asia-markets story showed Nikkei futures at 50,870, pointing to a gap-down of more than 2,500 points from Friday's cash close
After Houthi attacks on Israel, Brent rose to $115.73 and WTI to $102.77
USD/JPY rose to 160.47 early in the week, and Reuters' projected range is now 158-162 with real intervention risk back in view
Reuters expects the Nikkei to open sharply lower today, with a 50,000-52,000 range
Reuters' weekly Tokyo-market preview also starts from a sell-first stance and a 50,000-53,000 range
AMI lifted its net-profit and dividend outlook and paired that with a buyback capped at 15.96% of shares outstanding
Japan Post announced cancellation of treasury stock equal to 5.54% of shares outstanding
TPR raised full-year ordinary-profit guidance by 19% and effectively increased the dividend
Shikoku Bank raised full-year ordinary-profit guidance by 7% and increased the annual dividend by 4 yen

watchlist.name: INPEX
watchlist.why_today: With WTI above $102 and USD/JPY above 160, this is still the most straightforward first-order beneficiary to monitor on the long side.
watchpoints: Can oil stay above the $103 area through Tokyo hours
Can the stock hold relative strength against a falling Nikkei
Does buying stay concentrated in upstream energy
falsifiers: WTI breaks back below $100 and reverses sharply
Intervention or panic selling drags even resource names lower
watchlist.red_team: It is the clearest war hedge, but the setup can reverse immediately on any headline shift.

sources.title: Oil prices jump after Yemeni Houthis attack Israel, widening Iran conflict
Stocks slide in Asia, Brent crude heads for record monthly rise
ＩＮＰＥＸや石油資源が続伸、イラン情勢へ警戒感強くＷＴＩ価格は９４ドル台に上昇◇

watchlist.name: Japan Post Holdings
watchlist.why_today: It plans to cancel 5.54% of shares outstanding. In a weak tape, that kind of supply-improvement story is easy to understand and easy to monitor.
watchpoints: Does the market reward the cancellation cleanly at the open
Can the stock hold the 1,800-yen area
Do value buyers step in on any early dip
falsifiers: Investors treat the cancellation as largely priced in
Broad index weakness overwhelms large-cap value names too
watchlist.red_team: The cancellation is large, but it is still a supply story and may not overpower a market washout.

sources.title: 日本郵政(6178) 自己株式の消却に関するお知らせ
本日注目の〖自社株買い〗銘柄 (27日大引け後 発表分)

watchlist.name: Advanced Media
watchlist.why_today: The company paired a large 15.96% buyback with a higher net-profit outlook and a higher dividend, giving the market a very visible package to price.
watchpoints: Can it keep trading above the 1,000-yen area
Does the size of the buyback dominate the initial read
Can it overcome the downgrade at the ordinary-profit line
falsifiers: The ordinary-profit downgrade becomes the main focus
Weakness across small-cap growth names takes over
watchlist.red_team: The buyback and net-profit story is strong, but investors can still question the quality of earnings.

sources.title: ＡＭＩ(3773) 連結業績予想の修正および期末配当予想の修正ならびに投資有価証券の売却に関するお知らせ
ＡＭＩ(3773) 自己株式取得に係る事項の決定に関するお知らせ
ＡＭＩが２６年３月期最終利益予想と配当予想を上方修正、自社株買いも発表

watchlist.name: TPR
watchlist.why_today: It lifted ordinary-profit guidance by 19% and effectively raised the dividend on a split-adjusted basis. For a weekend carry-over, the numbers are clear.
watchpoints: Does the market reward both the revision and the dividend
Can it outperform the wider auto-parts group
falsifiers: The move is reduced to an FX-assisted earnings revision
Cyclical selling pressure resumes in auto parts
watchlist.red_team: A lot of the upgrade still leans on yen weakness, so the setup is not fully insulated from macro reversal.

sources.title: TPR(6463) 業績予想の修正および配当予想の修正に関するお知らせ
ＴＰＲは４日続伸、今期業績・配当予想を上方修正

watchlist.name: Shikoku Bank
watchlist.why_today: It raised both earnings guidance and the year-end dividend, adding a fresh numerical catalyst to the regional-bank theme.
watchpoints: Can it maintain relative strength within the regional-bank group
Does the higher payout keep attracting opening demand
Can volume remain active after the auction
falsifiers: The stock is swallowed by a generic bank-sector move
Most of the news has already been digested
watchlist.red_team: Its stock-specific edge can fade quickly if the regional-bank theme turns too broad.

sources.title: 四国銀(8387) 業績予想及び配当予想の修正（増配）に関するお知らせ
四国銀は後場上げ幅を拡大、資金利益など増え最終益・期末配当予想を引き上げ

deep_dive.title: INPEX: the key question is whether the clearest oil beneficiary can keep leading in a market washout

lines: The cleanest long idea into Monday's open is still the first-order oil beneficiary rather than a broad index rebound.
In a deeply risk-off tape, relative strength in upstream energy can matter more than ordinary earnings narratives.
Reuters' Asia-markets piece had Nikkei futures at 50,870 while Brent reached $115.33 and WTI $102.52, a combination that leaves Japan with few obvious winners.
Houthi attacks on Israel widened the conflict again, keeping crude bid into the Tokyo open.
Can WTI stay around or above the $100 area and allow INPEX to keep relative strength against the Nikkei.
Does volume remain concentrated in the stock rather than spread indiscriminately across all resource names.
Crude falls back below $100 and peace expectations suddenly strengthen.
Intervention or forced selling pulls down even the resource pocket of the market.
It is the clearest war hedge, but the premise can reverse immediately on any geopolitical headline.
If the whole market turns into liquidity-driven liquidation, fundamentals will matter less than positioning.

sources.title: Oil prices jump after Yemeni Houthis attack Israel, widening Iran conflict
Stocks slide in Asia, Brent crude heads for record monthly rise
ＮＹ原油時間外 103ドルに乗せる フーシ派がイスラエル攻撃開始

no_trade_conditions: If the Nikkei breaks materially below 51,000 and forced selling becomes the dominant tape
If WTI breaks back below $100 and the resource-outperformance premise collapses
If USD/JPY enters intervention-style volatility and opening price discovery stops functioning normally
If the top catalyst names such as Japan Post and Advanced Media fail to hold their opening premiums and are absorbed by the index selloff
content.disclaimer: This is a pre-open market digest, not investment advice. Please make your own final decisions.
